notes: Two bailout tiers, offered when budget < 0 at end of season. Each tier offers two choices: LOAN (budget injection + binding contract) or GRANT (budget injection + upfront views loss). Terms escalate from tier 1 to tier 2. Bailouts are capped at two per run.

tiers.name: First Lifeline
tiers.desc: The board is still rooting for you. One last chance before the restructuring team arrives.

loan.name: Parent Company Loan
loan.desc: An immediate cash injection from the parent company. Hit a modest views target over the next 3 seasons or pay it back with interest.

grant.name: Arts Council Emergency Subsidy
grant.desc: A public arts body will fund you  -  but only if you air some low-rated public-interest programming this season. No ongoing obligations, but your ratings cushion will shrink.

tiers.name: Last Resort
tiers.desc: The CFO is on the phone. This is it. Miss the target and the lawyers move in.

loan.name: Investor Emergency Injection
loan.desc: A venture-capital investor will bail you out  -  but they want a turnaround story. Reach a demanding views target in just 2 seasons or the repayment clause destroys your budget.

grant.name: Emergency Broadcasting Grant
grant.desc: A government emergency arts fund will cover your deficit  -  in exchange for airing a full season of grant-mandated content. Your ratings take a serious hit.
